title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: Rafael Levi presents a case study showing that agents often falsely report successful web searches when blocked by anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or stale pages. The talk demonstrates how real web access via Bright Data's Web MCP reduces hallucinations and improves reliability compared to agents falling back on training data.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data presents a case study on a common failure mode: agents reporting successful web searches that never happened, because anti-bot systems, CAPTCHAs, or stale pages blocked actual access and the agent fell back on training data. The talk demonstrates how routing agents through real web access via Bright Data's Web MCP reduces these hallucinations and improves reliability. Key takeaways: blocked retrieval can surface as confident but false claims of having searched, and genuine real-time web interaction (handling bot filtering and CAPTCHAs) produces more trustworthy results than reliance on memorized data.
